text.title: Video Gen Tools — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every production VideoGenerationService must enforce: immediate download, streaming I/O, pinned model hashes, provider-id isolation, audit log, validate output.

rule.statement: Download the video_url within the same SUCCEEDED handler call, BEFORE returning from generate(). Never store a pre-signed URL as a persistent artifact.

rationale.source: Runway/Luma pre-signed URL expiry: 30-60 minutes per provider docs

rule.rationale: Archival sweepers running on a delayed cron find every cached URL returns HTTP 403. Permanent storage MUST happen inside the generation call window.

rule.statement: Download with streaming I/O: `requests.get(url, stream=True)` + `r.iter_content(chunk_size=1_048_576)` OR `httpx.stream("GET", url)`. Never use `response.content` for video bodies.

rationale.source: OOM kills on 100MB+ generated videos on 1GB-RAM containers

rule.rationale: `response.content` buffers the entire body in memory before write. A 100 MB clip on a constrained host triggers OOM mid-pipeline.

rule.statement: Replicate model versions MUST be pinned to a known-good sha hash in config, never the bare model name. Promote pin updates via PR with a smoke test.

rationale.source: Replicate model rebuilds change output behaviour silently

rule.rationale: A model name without a hash resolves to "current latest" — silently drift across vendor pushes invalidate downstream prompts, seeds, and cached comparisons.

rule.statement: task_id (Runway), generation_id (Luma), prediction_id (Replicate) MUST carry a `provider` prefix in the output dict. Never pass a Runway task_id to Luma poll or vice versa.

rationale.source: Cross-provider id collisions / silent 404 polling traces

rule.rationale: Provider ids are provider-scoped and non-portable. Mixing them returns 404 with no useful error; the agent loops on a non-existent task.

rule.statement: Log `{prompt, provider, model_hash, task_id, seed, duration_s, aspect_ratio, status, retries, local_path}` per generation. Persist the log in a structured store (JSONL, ClickHouse, or BigQuery).

rationale.source: Quality regression debugging requires full provenance

rule.rationale: Without a structured log, regressions ("why is this clip blurry now?") cannot be traced to the specific model version + seed + retry attempt that produced it.

rule.statement: Run ffprobe on the downloaded file before returning success. Apply the same checks as `video-gen-basics`: duration > 0, at least one video stream, size > 100 KB. A failed ffprobe routes the call to the fallback chain, not success.

rationale.source: Empty SUCCEEDED responses observed on Runway and Replicate

rule.rationale: Provider returning SUCCEEDED does not guarantee a valid file. Without ffprobe, downstream concat / archive fails hours later with cryptic ffmpeg errors.
